infobox.value: [Royal Italian Army](/wiki/Royal_Italian_Army "Royal Italian Army")  [Italian Army](/wiki/Italian_Army "Italian Army")

[Expedition of the Thousand](/wiki/Expedition_of_the_Thousand "Expedition of the Thousand") [Third Italian War of Independence](/wiki/Third_Italian_War_of_Independence "Third Italian War of Independence") [World War I](/wiki/World_War_I "World War I") [World War II](/wiki/World_War_II "World War II")

was one of three corps the [Italian Army](/wiki/Italian_Army "Italian Army") fielded during the [Cold War](/wiki/Cold_War "Cold War"). Based in the regions of [Veneto](/wiki/Veneto "Veneto") and [Friuli-Venezia Giulia](/wiki/Friuli-Venezia_Giulia "Friuli-Venezia Giulia") the corps was the army's main combat force. The 5th Army Corps was arrayed close to the [Yugoslavian](/wiki/Yugoslavia "Yugoslavia") border and tasked with meeting any [Warsaw Pact](/wiki/Warsaw_Pact "Warsaw Pact") forces that crossed the border. On the left flank of the corps the [4th Alpine Army Corps](/wiki/4th_Alpine_Army_Corps "4th Alpine Army Corps") was tasked with blocking the [Alpine](/wiki/Alps "Alps") passes and in the rear of the corps the [3rd Army Corps](/wiki/3rd_Army_Corps_(Italy) "3rd Army Corps (Italy)") served as operational reserve. After the end of the Cold War the corps was reduced in size and on 1 October 1997 it became the 1st Defence Forces Command (COMFOD 1°). In 2013 the COMFOD 1° was disbanded and its function and brigades taken over by the [20th Infantry Division Friuli](/wiki/20th_Infantry_Division_Friuli "20th Infantry Division Friuli") in Florence.

Military unit

The **V Army Corps** was one of three corps the [Italian Army](/wiki/Italian_Army "Italian Army") fielded during the [Cold War](/wiki/Cold_War "Cold War"). Based in the regions of [Veneto](/wiki/Veneto "Veneto") and [Friuli-Venezia Giulia](/wiki/Friuli-Venezia_Giulia "Friuli-Venezia Giulia") the corps was the army's main combat force. The 5th Army Corps was arrayed close to the [Yugoslavian](/wiki/Yugoslavia "Yugoslavia") border and tasked with meeting any [Warsaw Pact](/wiki/Warsaw_Pact "Warsaw Pact") forces that crossed the border. On the left flank of the corps the [4th Alpine Army Corps](/wiki/4th_Alpine_Army_Corps "4th Alpine Army Corps") was tasked with blocking the [Alpine](/wiki/Alps "Alps") passes and in the rear of the corps the [3rd Army Corps](/wiki/3rd_Army_Corps_(Italy) "3rd Army Corps (Italy)") served as operational reserve. After the end of the Cold War the corps was reduced in size and on 1 October 1997 it became the 1st Defence Forces Command (COMFOD 1°). In 2013 the COMFOD 1° was disbanded and its function and brigades taken over by the [20th Infantry Division Friuli](/wiki/20th_Infantry_Division_Friuli "20th Infantry Division Friuli") in Florence.

## History

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=V_Army_Corps_(Italy)&action=edit&section=1 "Edit section: History")]

### Origins

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=V_Army_Corps_(Italy)&action=edit&section=2 "Edit section: Origins")]

The history of the 5th Army Corps begins with [Garibaldis](/wiki/Giuseppe_Garibaldi "Giuseppe Garibaldi") [Expedition of the Thousand](/wiki/Expedition_of_the_Thousand "Expedition of the Thousand"). After Garibaldi scored victory after victory in his conquest of the [Kingdom of the Two Sicilies](/wiki/Kingdom_of_the_Two_Sicilies "Kingdom of the Two Sicilies"), the [Victor Emmanuel II](/wiki/Victor_Emmanuel_II_of_Italy "Victor Emmanuel II of Italy") of [Sardinia](/wiki/Kingdom_of_Sardinia "Kingdom of Sardinia") decided to march south to secure the conquered territory for himself and thus [unite Italy](/wiki/Italian_unification "Italian unification"). The V Army Corps was raised and marched south with the main Army where it fought in the battles of San Giuliano on 26 October 1860 and Garigliano on 29 October 1860 and participated in the [siege of Gaeta](/wiki/Siege_of_Gaeta_(1860) "Siege of Gaeta (1860)").

After the war the corps was garrisoned in [Florence](/wiki/Florence "Florence"), the capital of the newly united Italy. It commanded two elite divisions: the 1st Division of the Line in Florence and the 15th Division of the Line in [Perugia](/wiki/Perugia "Perugia"). Both divisions were made up by [Grenadier](/wiki/Grenadier "Grenadier") regiments.

At the outbreak of the [third Italian war of independence](/wiki/Third_Italian_War_of_Independence "Third Italian War of Independence") the V Army Corps remained at first in Florence, but when the main Italian army failed to break through the Austrian [Quadrilatero](/wiki/Quadrilatero "Quadrilatero") fortress system south of [Lake Garda](/wiki/Lake_Garda "Lake Garda") in the [Battle of Custoza](/wiki/Battle_of_Custoza_(1866) "Battle of Custoza (1866)"), the corps marched six divisions over the [Apennine](/wiki/Apennine_Mountains "Apennine Mountains") mountains, joined up with General [Enrico Cialdinis](/wiki/Enrico_Cialdini "Enrico Cialdini") eight divisions of the [IV Army Corps](/wiki/4th_Alpine_Army_Corps "4th Alpine Army Corps") in the [Romagna](/wiki/Emilia-Romagna "Emilia-Romagna") and crossed the lower [Po](/wiki/Po_(river) "Po (river)") and [Adige](/wiki/Adige "Adige") rivers in force on 11 July 1866. Bypassing the Austrian fortresses and main army on his left flank Cialdini marched his army all through the [Veneto](/wiki/Veneto "Veneto"), dispatching one division under [Giacomo Medici](/wiki/Giacomo_Medici_(general) "Giacomo Medici (general)") to invade [Trentino](/wiki/Trentino "Trentino") and cut the Austrian line of retreat and three divisions under [Raffaele Cadorna](/wiki/Raffaele_Cadorna "Raffaele Cadorna") to march at speed to the city of [Trieste](/wiki/Trieste "Trieste"). Cialdinis army reached the [Isonzo](/wiki/So%C4%8Da "Soča") river on 24 July 1866.

### World War I

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=V_Army_Corps_(Italy)&action=edit&section=3 "Edit section: World War I")]

[![](//upload.wikimedia.org/wikipedia/commons/thumb/9/95/Guerra_Altipiani_Dopo_Assalto.jpg/500px-Guerra_Altipiani_Dopo_Assalto.jpg)](/wiki/File:Guerra_Altipiani_Dopo_Assalto.jpg)

The [Asiago](/wiki/Asiago "Asiago") plateau after the [Battle of Asiago](/wiki/Battle_of_Asiago "Battle of Asiago")

After the [Italian declaration of war](/wiki/Italian_Front_(World_War_I) "Italian Front (World War I)") against the [Austrian Empire](/wiki/Austria-Hungary "Austria-Hungary") on 23 May 1915 the V Army Corps under Lieutenant General [Florenzio Aliprandi](/w/index.php?title=Florenzio_Aliprandi&action=edit&redlink=1 "Florenzio Aliprandi (page does not exist)") as part of the 1st Army advanced into [Trentino](/wiki/Trentino "Trentino"). On the corps left flank the [III Army Corps](/wiki/3rd_Army_Corps_(Italy) "3rd Army Corps (Italy)") advanced through the [Valtellina](/wiki/Valtellina "Valtellina"), [Camonica](/wiki/Val_Camonica "Val Camonica") [Trompia](/wiki/Trompia "Trompia") and [Chiese](/wiki/Chiese "Chiese") valleys and along the Western shore of [Lake Garda](/wiki/Lake_Garda "Lake Garda"). The ultimate goal of the 1st Army was to reach the city of [Trento](/wiki/Trento "Trento"). Although numerically superior the corps failed to reach the city of Trento and its advance through the [Adige](/wiki/Adige "Adige") and [Valsugana](/wiki/Sugana_Valley "Sugana Valley") valleys and over the [Asiago](/wiki/Asiago "Asiago") plateau soon bogged down in determined Austrian resistance. The corps consisted of the 9th and 15th Division of the Line (Infantry), the 34th Territorial Division, the 2nd, 4th and 8th [Bersaglieri](/wiki/Bersaglieri "Bersaglieri") regiments and the [6th Alpini Regiment](/wiki/6th_Alpini_Regiment "6th Alpini Regiment"). All soldiers of the 6th Alpini Regiment had been recruited from valleys in the area of operations and thus fought in a territory they knew perfectly well. However the 6th Alpini Regiment was never employed as a whole, but single companies or battalions were given specific mountain summits, ridges or passes to conquer and hold.

After the initial advance had been brought to a halt by the [Austro-Hungarian Army](/wiki/Austro-Hungarian_Army "Austro-Hungarian Army") the corps dug in and, although fierce fighting continued the front in the corps' sector remained almost unchanged until 15 May 1916 when Austria unleashed its [first major offensive on the Italian front](/wiki/Battle_of_Asiago "Battle of Asiago") on the [Asiago](/wiki/Asiago "Asiago") plateau. The V Corps was badly mauled by the Austrian superiority in men and material but did not break. As the Russians began the [Brusilov Offensive](/wiki/Brusilov_Offensive "Brusilov Offensive"), on 4 June the Austro-Hungarian High Command decided do end its offensive operations in Italy before its strategic goal - a breakthrough into the [Venetian](/wiki/Veneto "Veneto") plains behind the main Italian Army - had been achieved.

After the end of the Austrian offensive the corps remained in the same area of operation until the end of the war.

### World War II

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=V_Army_Corps_(Italy)&action=edit&section=4 "Edit section: World War II")]

After the war the V Army Corps was garrisoned in [Trieste](/wiki/Trieste "Trieste") and consisted of the [12th Infantry Division "Timavo"](/wiki/12th_Infantry_Division_%22Timavo%22 "12th Infantry Division \"Timavo\"") in Trieste and the [15th Infantry Division "Bergamo"](/wiki/15th_Infantry_Division_%22Bergamo%22 "15th Infantry Division \"Bergamo\"") in [Pula](/wiki/Pula "Pula"). At the outbreak of [World War II](/wiki/World_War_II "World War II") the corps was guarding the border with [Yugoslavia](/wiki/Yugoslavia "Yugoslavia"). Headquartered in [Pivka](/wiki/Pivka "Pivka") it consisted of the [12th Infantry Division "Sassari"](/wiki/12th_Infantry_Division_%22Sassari%22 "12th Infantry Division \"Sassari\""), [15th Infantry Division "Bergamo"](/wiki/15th_Infantry_Division_%22Bergamo%22 "15th Infantry Division \"Bergamo\"") and [57th Infantry Division "Lombardia"](/wiki/57th_Infantry_Division_%22Lombardia%22 "57th Infantry Division \"Lombardia\"").

On 6 April 1941, under command of [Riccardo Balocco](/wiki/Riccardo_Balocco "Riccardo Balocco"), it participated in the [Axis invasion of Yugoslavia](/wiki/Axis_invasion_of_Yugoslavia "Axis invasion of Yugoslavia"). After the end of combat operations the corps took up garrison duties along the [Dalmatian](/wiki/Dalmatia "Dalmatia") coast, but found itself quickly fighting [Yugoslav partisans](/wiki/Yugoslav_partisans "Yugoslav partisans"). The corps (commanded by General [Renato Coturri](/wiki/Renato_Coturri "Renato Coturri"), General [Alessandro Gloria](/wiki/Alessandro_Gloria "Alessandro Gloria") from January 1943 and by General [Antonio Scuero](/wiki/Antonio_Scuero "Antonio Scuero") from May 1943) was garrisoned in [Crikvenica](/wiki/Crikvenica "Crikvenica") at the time and consisted of the [153rd Infantry Division "Macerata"](/wiki/153rd_Infantry_Division_%22Macerata%22 "153rd Infantry Division \"Macerata\"") in [Delnice](/wiki/Delnice "Delnice"), [154th Infantry Division "Murge"](/wiki/154th_Infantry_Division_%22Murge%22 "154th Infantry Division \"Murge\"") in [Sinj](/wiki/Sinj "Sinj") and the XIV Coastal Brigade in Crikvenica.  
After Germany invaded Italy following the [Italian-Allied armistice](/wiki/Armistice_of_Cassibile "Armistice of Cassibile") in September 1943 the corps was disbanded by the Germans.

### Cold War

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=V_Army_Corps_(Italy)&action=edit&section=6 "Edit section: Cold War")]

In 1945 the V Territorial Military Command was activated in [Padua](/wiki/Padua "Padua") which at the time controlled only the [5th Alpini Regiment](/wiki/5th_Alpini_Regiment "5th Alpini Regiment"). But already by 1947 the command had grown to include the [Infantry Division "Folgore"](/wiki/Infantry_Division_%22Folgore%22 "Infantry Division \"Folgore\"") in [Treviso](/wiki/Treviso "Treviso") and the [Infantry Division "Mantova"](/wiki/Infantry_Division_%22Mantova%22 "Infantry Division \"Mantova\"") in [Gorizia](/wiki/Gorizia "Gorizia"). Both divisions were units of the [Italian Co-Belligerent Army](/wiki/Italian_Co-Belligerent_Army "Italian Co-Belligerent Army") and had served on the Allied side during the [Italian campaign](/wiki/Italian_Campaign_(World_War_II) "Italian Campaign (World War II)"). In 1948 the command was augmented with the [Armored Brigade "Ariete"](/wiki/Armored_Brigade_%22Ariete%22 "Armored Brigade \"Ariete\"") in [Pordenone](/wiki/Pordenone "Pordenone") and in 1949 with the [Alpine Brigade "Julia"](/wiki/Alpine_Brigade_%22Julia%22 "Alpine Brigade \"Julia\"") in [Cividale del Friuli](/wiki/Cividale_del_Friuli "Cividale del Friuli"). The command was tasked with defending the Italian Eastern borders: the Mantova was to defend the city of Gorizia, while the Julia covered the commands left flank in the [Carnic Alps](/wiki/Carnic_Alps "Carnic Alps"). The motorized Folgore was tasked to advance to the [Free Territory of Trieste](/wiki/Free_Territory_of_Trieste "Free Territory of Trieste") and support the [Trieste United States Troops](/wiki/Trieste_United_States_Troops "Trieste United States Troops"), while the Ariete with its [M4 Sherman](/wiki/M4_Sherman "M4 Sherman") tanks, [M3 Half-tracks](/wiki/M3_Half-track "M3 Half-track") and [M7 Priests](/wiki/M7_Priest "M7 Priest") served as the commands mobile reserve. To cover its right flank against enemy amphibious landings the command raised the [Lagunari](/wiki/Lagunari "Lagunari") troops in Venice on 15 January 1951.

On 1 May 1952 the V Territorial Military Command was renamed as V Army Corps and as the [West-East confrontation](/wiki/Cold_War "Cold War") became more dire the Ariete tripled in size and became the [Armored Division "Ariete"](/wiki/Armored_Division_%22Ariete%22 "Armored Division \"Ariete\"") on 1 October 1952. division also exchanged its Sherman tanks for more powerful [M26 Pershings](/wiki/M26_Pershing "M26 Pershing"). On 30 September the corps 1953 moved to [Vittorio Veneto](/wiki/Vittorio_Veneto "Vittorio Veneto") and ceded command of the Julia to the new Carnia-Cadore Troops Command, which covered the V Army Corps left flank.

Over the next years the corps added further units to keep up with the geopolitical situation of the [Cold War](/wiki/Cold_War "Cold War"): the Ariete was further increased and received [M47 Patton](/wiki/M47_Patton "M47 Patton") tanks and [M113 armored personnel carriers](/wiki/M113_armored_personnel_carrier "M113 armored personnel carrier"), as well as one artillery group armed with [M44 self-propelled howitzers](/wiki/M44_Self_Propelled_Howitzer "M44 Self Propelled Howitzer") and one artillery group armed with [M110 self-propelled howitzers](/wiki/M110_howitzer "M110 howitzer"). The Mantova and Folgore infantry divisions were each increased by one infantry regiment and both received a tank battalion with M47 Patton tanks. In [Trieste](/wiki/Trieste "Trieste") the [151st Infantry Regiment "Sassari"](/wiki/151st_Infantry_Regiment_%22Sassari%22 "151st Infantry Regiment \"Sassari\"") and the [14th Field Artillery Regiment](/wiki/14th_Field_Artillery_Regiment_(Italy) "14th Field Artillery Regiment (Italy)") formed the [Trieste Troops Command](/wiki/Trieste_Troops_Command "Trieste Troops Command") tasked with defending the city. At the same time the Lagunari regiment in Venice grew to three amphibious battalions and one tank battalion with M47 Patton tanks. In Gorizia on 1 January 1959 the mechanized [Cavalry Brigade "Pozzuolo del Friuli"](/wiki/Cavalry_Brigade_%22Pozzuolo_del_Friuli%22 "Cavalry Brigade \"Pozzuolo del Friuli\"") activated with two mechanized, one tank battalion and one self-propelled artillery group, to replace the Mantova, which had moved its headquarters to [Udine](/wiki/Udine "Udine").

The most significant addition to the corps was the [III Missile Brigade](/wiki/3rd_Missile_Brigade_%22Aquileia%22 "3rd Missile Brigade \"Aquileia\""). The brigade activated on 1 October 1959 in [Vicenza](/wiki/Vicenza "Vicenza") and initially consisted of one missile artillery regiment with two missile groups, each of which was armed with eight [Honest John](/wiki/MGR-1_Honest_John "MGR-1 Honest John") missile launchers. The [MGR-1 Honest John](/wiki/MGR-1_Honest_John "MGR-1 Honest John") was a [nuclear](/wiki/Nuclear_weapon "Nuclear weapon")-capable [surface-to-surface missile](/wiki/Surface-to-surface_missile "Surface-to-surface missile") intended to destroy the large Warsaw Pact armored formations. By the 1963, when the brigades headquarters was moved to [Portogruaro](/wiki/Portogruaro "Portogruaro"), the brigade contained four missile groups and one group with [M115 howitzers](/wiki/M115_howitzer "M115 howitzer").

The corps - along with the [3rd Army Corps](/wiki/3rd_Army_Corps_(Italy) "3rd Army Corps (Italy)") and [4th Army Corps](/wiki/COMALP "COMALP") was part of [NATOs](/wiki/NATO "NATO") [Allied Land Forces Southern Europe](/wiki/Allied_Land_Forces_Southern_Europe "Allied Land Forces Southern Europe") Command (LANDSOUTH) in [Verona](/wiki/Verona "Verona").

Combat brigades of the 5th Army Corps in 1986

[![](//upload.wikimedia.org/wikipedia/commons/thumb/f/f7/Italian_Army_-_5th_Army_Corps_-_1989.png/960px-Italian_Army_-_5th_Army_Corps_-_1989.png)](/wiki/File:Italian_Army_-_5th_Army_Corps_-_1989.png)

Structure of the 5th Army Corps in 1989 at the End of the [Cold War](/wiki/Cold_War "Cold War") (click to enlarge)

In 1986 the Italian Army abolished the divisional level and realigned its forces to counter a possible [Warsaw Pact](/wiki/Warsaw_Pact "Warsaw Pact") attack better. The 5th Army Corps was to destroy enemy forces within the region of [Friuli-Venezia Giulia](/wiki/Friuli-Venezia_Giulia "Friuli-Venezia Giulia"), while the [4th Alpine Army Corps](/wiki/COMALP "COMALP") covered the V Army Corps' left flank and the [3rd Army Corps](/wiki/3rd_Army_Corps_(Italy) "3rd Army Corps (Italy)") was the operational reserve. As two of the V Army Corps' mechanized brigades were based outside its area of operation, the V Corps lost these two brigades to the 3rd Army Corps: the [Mechanized Brigade "Brescia"](/wiki/Mechanized_Brigade_%22Brescia%22 "Mechanized Brigade \"Brescia\"") in [Brescia](/wiki/Brescia "Brescia") and the [Mechanized Brigade "Trieste"](/wiki/Mechanized_Brigade_%22Trieste%22 "Mechanized Brigade \"Trieste\"") in [Bologna](/wiki/Bologna "Bologna"). As the Ariete and Mantova divisions carried historically significant names - the Ariete having fought bravely in the [Siege of Tobruk](/wiki/Siege_of_Tobruk "Siege of Tobruk") and [Battle of Gazala](/wiki/Battle_of_Gazala "Battle of Gazala") and fought to annihilation in the [Second Battle of El Alamein](/wiki/Second_Battle_of_El_Alamein "Second Battle of El Alamein"), and the Mantova as one of the divisions that fought in the [Italian Co-Belligerent Army](/wiki/Italian_Co-Belligerent_Army "Italian Co-Belligerent Army") to liberate Italy from [Nazi-occupation](/wiki/Italian_Social_Republic "Italian Social Republic") - the army decided to rename the 132nd Armored Brigade "Manin" as 132nd Armored Brigade "Ariete" and the Mechanized Brigade "Isonzo" as Mechanized Brigade "Mantova". Although the Folgore divisions carried and equally significant name the army decided to transfer the traditions of the Folgore division to the existing [Paratroopers Brigade "Folgore"](/wiki/Paratroopers_Brigade_%22Folgore%22 "Paratroopers Brigade \"Folgore\"").

Each mechanized brigade fielded about 4,700 men, while the armored brigades fielded about 3,400 men. Together with the corps' support units the entire corps fielded over 60,000 men.

### After the Cold War

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=V_Army_Corps_(Italy)&action=edit&section=8 "Edit section: After the Cold War")]

With the end of the Cold War the Italian Army began a decade long reduction of its forces. The first brigade to disband was the *Mameli* on 1 April 1991, followed by the *Vittorio Veneto* on 31 July 1991. The units subordinated to these brigades were mostly disbanded, with a few units joining other brigades. The *Garibaldi* completed its move to [Caserta](/wiki/Caserta "Caserta") in the South of Italy on 1 July 1991 and left the 5th Army Corps on that day. Finally on 1 December 1991 the *Aquileia* was reduced to 3rd Artillery Regiment *Aquileia*, which was then disbanded in September 1992. Over the next few years the Trieste Troops Command and the Amphibious Troops Command were disbanded.

In 1997 the army undertook the next big reform process and the 5th Army Corps saw its *Gorizia* and *Mantova* brigades disbanded on 30 August 1997, but it received the [Mechanized Brigade *Centauro*](/wiki/Centauro_Armored_Brigade "Centauro Armored Brigade") from the [3rd Army Corps](/wiki/3rd_Army_Corps_(Italy) "3rd Army Corps (Italy)"). On 1 October 1997 the 5th Army Corps changed its name and became the 1st Defence Forces Command (*1° Comando Forze di Difesa* or COMFOD 1°). The new structure of the corps was:

  + [![](//upload.wikimedia.org/wikipedia/commons/thumb/0/03/CoA_mil_ITA_rgt_fanteria_007.png/40px-CoA_mil_ITA_rgt_fanteria_007.png)](/wiki/File:CoA_mil_ITA_rgt_fanteria_007.png) [7th Infantry Regiment "Cuneo"](/w/index.php?title=7th_Infantry_Regiment_%22Cuneo%22&action=edit&redlink=1 "7th Infantry Regiment \"Cuneo\" (page does not exist)") (1x training battalion), in Udine
  + [![](//upload.wikimedia.org/wikipedia/commons/thumb/c/c6/CoA_mil_ITA_rgt_fanteria_011.png/40px-CoA_mil_ITA_rgt_fanteria_011.png)](/wiki/File:CoA_mil_ITA_rgt_fanteria_011.png) [11th Infantry Regiment "Casale"](/wiki/11th_Infantry_Regiment_%22Casale%22 "11th Infantry Regiment \"Casale\"") (1x training battalion), in [Casale Monferrato](/wiki/Casale_Monferrato "Casale Monferrato")
  + [![](//upload.wikimedia.org/wikipedia/commons/thumb/e/e5/CoA_mil_ITA_rgt_genio_03.png/40px-CoA_mil_ITA_rgt_genio_03.png)](/wiki/File:CoA_mil_ITA_rgt_genio_03.png) [3rd Engineer Regiment](/wiki/3rd_Engineer_Regiment_(Italy) "3rd Engineer Regiment (Italy)") (1x battalion), in [Udine](/wiki/Udine "Udine")
  + [![](//upload.wikimedia.org/wikipedia/commons/thumb/0/0a/CoA_mil_ITA_rgt_trasmissioni_07.png/40px-CoA_mil_ITA_rgt_trasmissioni_07.png)](/wiki/File:CoA_mil_ITA_rgt_trasmissioni_07.png) [7th Signal Regiment](/wiki/7th_Signal_Regiment_(Italy) "7th Signal Regiment (Italy)") (1x battalion), in [Sacile](/wiki/Sacile "Sacile")

On 1 December 2000 the [Projection Forces Command](/wiki/3rd_Army_Corps_(Italy) "3rd Army Corps (Italy)") (COMFOP) ceded two of its brigades - the [Paratroopers Brigade "Folgore"](/wiki/Paratroopers_Brigade_%22Folgore%22 "Paratroopers Brigade \"Folgore\"") and [Airmobile Brigade "Friuli"](/wiki/Airmobile_Brigade_%22Friuli%22 "Airmobile Brigade \"Friuli\"") to the 1st Defence Forces Command. In 2002 the command changed composition for the last time: the Centauro was disbanded on 5 October 2002 and all support units were either disbanded or transferred to other commands. However, in the same year the command began to build up a deployable division-level headquarters. Said headquarters became operational in October 2005 and was given the name Division Command "Mantova".
